T1033.name: System Owner/User Discovery
T1033.shortDescription: Adversaries may attempt to identify the primary user, currently logged in user, set of users that commonly uses a system, or whether a user is actively using the system.

T1110.001.name: Password Guessing
T1110.001.shortDescription: Adversaries with no prior knowledge of legitimate credentials within the system or environment may guess passwords to attempt access to accounts.

T1590.005.name: IP Addresses
T1590.005.shortDescription: Adversaries may gather the victim's IP addresses that can be used during targeting.

T1548.003.name: Sudo and Sudo Caching
T1548.003.shortDescription: Adversaries may perform sudo caching and/or use the sudoers file to elevate privileges.

T1003.002.name: Security Account Manager
T1003.002.shortDescription: Adversaries may attempt to extract credential material from the Security Account Manager (SAM) database either through in-memory techniques or through the Windows Registry where the SAM database is...

T1574.011.name: Services Registry Permissions Weakness
T1574.011.shortDescription: Adversaries may execute their own malicious payloads by hijacking the Registry entries used by services.

T1596.001.name: DNS/Passive DNS
T1596.001.shortDescription: Adversaries may search DNS data for information about victims that can be used during targeting.

T1552.005.name: Cloud Instance Metadata API
T1552.005.shortDescription: Adversaries may attempt to access the Cloud Instance Metadata API to collect credentials and other sensitive data.

T1087.002.name: Domain Account
T1087.002.shortDescription: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of domain accounts.

T1053.003.name: Cron
T1053.003.shortDescription: Adversaries may abuse the cron utility to perform task scheduling for initial or recurring execution of malicious code.

T1069.002.name: Domain Groups
T1069.002.shortDescription: Adversaries may attempt to find domain-level groups and permission settings.

T1530.name: Data from Cloud Storage
T1530.shortDescription: Adversaries may access data from cloud storage.

T1135.name: Network Share Discovery
T1135.shortDescription: Adversaries may look for folders and drives shared on remote systems as a means of identifying sources of information to gather as a precursor for Collection and to identify potential systems of in...

T1558.004.name: AS-REP Roasting
T1558.004.shortDescription: Adversaries may reveal credentials of accounts that have disabled Kerberos preauthentication by [Password Cracking](https://attack.

T1190.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
T1190.shortDescription: Adversaries may attempt to exploit a weakness in an Internet-facing host or system to initially access a network.

T1572.name: Protocol Tunneling
T1572.shortDescription: Adversaries may tunnel network communications to and from a victim system within a separate protocol to avoid detection/network filtering and/or enable access to otherwise unreachable systems.

T1595.002.name: Vulnerability Scanning
T1595.002.shortDescription: Adversaries may scan victims for vulnerabilities that can be used during targeting.

T1505.003.name: Web Shell
T1505.003.shortDescription: Adversaries may backdoor web servers with web shells to establish persistent access to systems.

T1552.004.name: Private Keys
T1552.004.shortDescription: Adversaries may search for private key certificate files on compromised systems for insecurely stored credentials.

T1003.001.name: LSASS Memory
T1003.001.shortDescription: Adversaries may attempt to access credential material stored in the process memory of the Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (LSASS).

T1548.001.name: Setuid and Setgid
T1548.001.shortDescription: An adversary may abuse configurations where an application has the setuid or setgid bits set in order to get code running in a different (and possibly more privileged) user’s context.

T1589.002.name: Email Addresses
T1589.002.shortDescription: Adversaries may gather email addresses that can be used during targeting.

T1558.001.name: Golden Ticket
T1558.001.shortDescription: Adversaries who have the KRBTGT account password hash may forge Kerberos ticket-granting tickets (TGT), also known as a golden ticket.

T1083.name: File and Directory Discovery
T1083.shortDescription: Adversaries may enumerate files and directories or may search in specific locations of a host or network share for certain information within a file system.

T1649.name: Steal or Forge Authentication Certificates
T1649.shortDescription: Adversaries may steal or forge certificates used for authentication to access remote systems or resources.

T1057.name: Process Discovery
T1057.shortDescription: Adversaries may attempt to get information about running processes on a system.

T1591.name: Gather Victim Org Information
T1591.shortDescription: Adversaries may gather information about the victim's organization that can be used during targeting.

T1069.001.name: Local Groups
T1069.001.shortDescription: Adversaries may attempt to find local system groups and permission settings.

T1059.004.name: Unix Shell
T1059.004.shortDescription: Adversaries may abuse Unix shell commands and scripts for execution.

T1592.002.name: Software
T1592.002.shortDescription: Adversaries may gather information about the victim's host software that can be used during targeting.

T1595.003.name: Wordlist Scanning
T1595.003.shortDescription: Adversaries may iteratively probe infrastructure using brute-forcing and crawling techniques.

T1574.009.name: Path Interception by Unquoted Path
T1574.009.shortDescription: Adversaries may execute their own malicious payloads by hijacking vulnerable file path references.

T1595.001.name: Scanning IP Blocks
T1595.001.shortDescription: Adversaries may scan victim IP blocks to gather information that can be used during targeting.

T1071.001.name: Web Protocols
T1071.001.shortDescription: Adversaries may communicate using application layer protocols associated with web traffic to avoid detection/network filtering by blending in with existing traffic.

T1018.name: Remote System Discovery
T1018.shortDescription: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of other systems by IP address, hostname, or other logical identifier on a network that may be used for Lateral Movement from the current system.

T1046.name: Network Service Discovery
T1046.shortDescription: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of services running on remote hosts and local network infrastructure devices, including those that may be vulnerable to remote software exploitation.

T1550.002.name: Pass the Hash
T1550.002.shortDescription: Adversaries may “pass the hash” using stolen password hashes to move laterally within an environment, bypassing normal system access controls.

T1648.name: Serverless Execution
T1648.shortDescription: Adversaries may abuse serverless computing, integration, and automation services to execute arbitrary code in cloud environments.

T1003.003.name: NTDS
T1003.003.shortDescription: Adversaries may attempt to access or create a copy of the Active Directory domain database in order to steal credential information, as well as obtain other information about domain members such as...

T1078.004.name: Cloud Accounts
T1078.004.shortDescription: Valid accounts in cloud environments may allow adversaries to perform actions to achieve Initial Access, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, or Defense Evasion.

T1558.003.name: Kerberoasting
T1558.003.shortDescription: Adversaries may abuse a valid Kerberos ticket-granting ticket (TGT) or sniff network traffic to obtain a ticket-granting service (TGS) ticket that may be vulnerable to [Brute Force](https://attack.

T1003.006.name: DCSync
T1003.006.shortDescription: Adversaries may attempt to access credentials and other sensitive information by abusing a Windows Domain Controller's application programming interface (API) to simulate the replication process fr...

T1588.005.name: Exploits
T1588.005.shortDescription: Adversaries may buy, steal, or download exploits that can be used during targeting.

T1090.001.name: Internal Proxy
T1090.001.shortDescription: Adversaries may use an internal proxy to direct command and control traffic between two or more systems in a compromised environment.
